  "findings": [
    "T-tapping on a Class B initiating device circuit (IDC) or notification appliance circuit (NAC)",
    "Loss of circuit monitoring integrity for branch devices beyond the T-tap point"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Locate and remove the T-tap splice",
    "Rewire the Class B circuit in a continuous daisy-chain configuration (in and out) to the end-of-line (EOL) resistor",
    "Verify circuit supervision and test for trouble signals upon removing any device"
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 72 strictly prohibits T-tapping on Class B circuits because it defeats the monitoring integrity of the circuit. If a wire breaks on a T-tapped branch, the fire alarm control panel cannot detect the open circuit, leaving downstream devices inoperable without generating a trouble signal.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 72 Chapter 12",
    "NFPA 72 Section 12.3"
  ]
}
